resources.string: CallShield needs to be set as your call screening app to block spam calls.
Permissions missing: %d. Open Settings to grant.
Calls and texts are actively protected.
Turn on call or SMS blocking to start screening.
Finish required permissions to activate protection.
Finish setup to activate the spam shield.
Enable the call screener so spam calls can be blocked.
Download the latest spam database to activate full protection.
Complete the core steps below to enable full protection.
Core protection is ready. Optional extras below add polish and visibility.
Download the latest spam database to activate blocking.
CallShield is set as the device call screener.
Set CallShield as the default call screener for live call blocking.
Call screening becomes required when call blocking is enabled.
Overlay permission is ready for caller ID popups.
Allow overlay access for floating caller ID alerts.
Notifications are enabled for alerts and recovery prompts.
Enable notifications for alerts, reminders, and recovery prompts.
Refresh the spam database before unknown campaigns slip through.
Refresh local spam records (%d) with the latest reports.
Review recent calls against your latest protection rules.
Scan your SMS inbox for messages that match spam rules.
Grant call log access before this scan can run.
Grant SMS access before this scan can run.
This will permanently delete the blocked call and SMS log. Total records: %d. This cannot be undone.
Use this workspace for exact numbers you never want to ring through, plus import and export of your personal blocklist.
Use wildcards or regex rules to catch repeat prefixes, suffixes, or campaign-shaped numbers without blocking them one by one.
Add targeted SMS phrases to catch recurring spam copy and tighten message filtering around known campaigns.
Numbers here always pass through. Mark critical contacts as emergency so they stay visually prominent and easy to review.
This is the read-only protection feed CallShield uses during screening. Review what is loaded locally and how specific numbers are labeled.
Add a number or import an existing personal blocklist to start building your manual protections.
Create a pattern rule when nuisance campaigns keep changing the last few digits.
Block an entire numbering range with a single pattern. Each # matches one digit; lengths must match exactly, so rules stay tight and predictable.
Paste a pattern like +33162###### to block an entire exchange in one rule.
Non-digit characters match themselves; # matches any single digit. The caller number must be exactly the same length as the pattern.
Enter a pattern containing at least one #.
Pattern would block more than 100,000,000 numbers. Narrow it down before saving.
This would also replace existing rule %1$s.
Let messaging, delivery, and rideshare notifications vouch for unknown callers. Requires notification access.
Alerts from these apps can vouch for unknown callers within 30 minutes. Turn off any source you don\'t want to influence call screening.
Add phrases like bank alert or claim prize when spam texts reuse the same wording.
Trusted numbers always pass through screening, even when aggressive protection is active.
Run a sync from Home to download the current spam feed before reviewing local database entries here.
Whitelisted numbers will never be blocked, even if they match spam rules.
Any SMS containing this keyword will be blocked.
Check any phone number against all 15 detection layers + ML
High-risk match. CallShield is %d%% confident this number should be blocked.
No spam rule matched. You can still save this number as safe or inspect its full detail view.
CallShield checks its local spam data, heuristics, and ML scorer on-device before showing a recommendation.
Understand why a number looks risky
Detection source, confidence, and matching details stay visible so each result feels explainable.
Keep good numbers out of aggressive filters, or report bad ones back to the community when something slips through.
Running local database, heuristics, and ML checks before we classify the result.
Fast hit from %1$s; checking remaining sources…
Set CallShield as your default call screening app.
Grant the access CallShield needs for spam detection, notifications, overlay caller ID, and call screening.
Reject calls from known spam numbers
Filter texts from spam numbers and content
Reject calls with hidden/no caller ID
Never block numbers in your contacts
Block calls failing carrier caller ID auth (Android 11+)
Short-circuit heuristic & ML blocks when the carrier has verified the caller (attestation A/B). User-blocked numbers always win.
Silence low-confidence blocks to voicemail instead of rejecting them, so you can review uncertain calls after the fact.
Flag calls matching your area code + exchange
VoIP ranges, premium rate, wangiri, rapid-fire
Spam keywords, phishing links, scam patterns
On-device gradient-boosted scorer. No internet required.
Block RCS spam via Notification Access. Covers Google/Samsung Messages.
Blocked calls go to voicemail silently. Your phone stays quiet while callers hear normal ringing.
Block all non-contact calls during set hours
Lower thresholds. More spam blocked, possible false positives. Contacts always safe.
Remove blocked log entries older than retention period
Export all blocked calls/SMS as a CSV file for analysis.
Includes blocklist, whitelist, wildcard rules, and keyword rules.
Free 250 lookups/month for carrier & line-type enrichment in the caller ID overlay. Never used for blocking.
Open-source spam blocker. No subscriptions, no tracking.
A quick read on what CallShield has intercepted recently.
Stats will appear after CallShield blocks its first calls or texts.
Spam trends, top offenders, area code heatmap
Detection engines, quiet hours, backup, cleanup
Verify all detection layers and permissions work
No crashes logged — CallShield hasn\'t recorded an uncaught exception.
Read the source, roadmap, and release history directly on GitHub.
Open an issue when something looks broken, confusing, or unfinished.
Support the project and help more people discover CallShield.
Send suspicious numbers back to the project with useful context.
Export the most recent local crash log for debugging. Nothing is auto-uploaded.
%1$s copied. Paste it into the form\'s phone-number field.
No browser available to open the report form.
Always ring through — bypasses blocklist, quiet hours, and aggressive mode.
Add your kid\'s school, your doctor, a family emergency line — any number that must ring through even when CallShield would otherwise block it.
This number was allowed through — no block reasoning to show.
Open-source spam call & text blocker with 15-layer detection engine + ML scorer. No API keys, no accounts, no tracking. Everything runs on-device.
All detection runs on-device. No data sent to any server.
Validates all detection layers and permissions are working correctly.
This diagnostic stays on-device and verifies the parts of CallShield that matter most before live calls or texts arrive.
Permissions and Android roles needed for screening.
Core spam engines, ML scoring, and local data readiness.
Optional extras like overlay caller ID and RCS monitoring.
Everything needed for day-to-day protection is connected and responding normally.
Review the checks below to restore full protection and premium extras.
Grant the missing permissions in Android app settings, then rerun the diagnostic.
Set CallShield as the default call screener so spam calls can be intercepted live.
Run a database sync from the dashboard or More screen so local protection data is available.
Review detection engine toggles in Settings and rerun this check.
Wait for the next refresh or run a manual sync if this remains empty.
Allow overlay access if you want floating caller ID alerts on incoming calls.
Enable notification or listener access if you want alerts and RCS filtering.
Not granted — feature may not work
CallShield is the default call screener
Not set — go to Settings to enable
Failed to detect premium rate number
ML scorer may be disabled or weights not loaded
False positive — threshold may be too low
Empty — will populate on next 30-min sync
Not granted — RCS filter won\'t work
Open-source spam call & text blocker with 15-layer detection + ML scorer. No API keys, no tracking.
CallShield needs access to your call log, contacts, and SMS to detect and block spam.
CallShield needs to be your default call screening app to block spam calls.
CallShield ships with a built-in protection snapshot and refreshes from GitHub when available. Hot data refreshes every 30 minutes.
No accounts, subscriptions, or tracking required.
Spam scoring and blocking decisions stay on your phone.
Bundled protection works immediately, then updates sharpen coverage over time.
Needed for scanning calls, checking contacts, and filtering SMS spam.
Lets CallShield show alert notifications and monitor RCS spam when enabled.
Shows a floating caller ID card on top of incoming calls.
Android will route incoming calls through CallShield before they ring.
This is the switch that turns on live call screening for unknown callers.
Call screening unavailable on this device
You can still use blocklists, scans, and lookup tools, but Android is not exposing the native screening role here.
Android could not open the call screening setup screen on this device.
CallShield starts with a bundled protection snapshot, so you are not waiting on a first sync.
GitHub-backed updates refresh local spam data whenever a newer bundle is available.
Hot campaign ranges refresh separately, helping short-lived robocall spikes get caught faster.
You can finish onboarding now and start using CallShield with live screening enabled.
You can finish now and tighten things later
CallShield will still open, and you can come back in Settings to complete any missing setup items.
Review your latest calls, refresh quickly, and drill straight into suspicious numbers.
Grant call log permission so CallShield can audit recent calls, surface spam matches, and open detail views from your history.
CallShield reads call history on-device so you can understand spam matches without leaving your recent-call view.
If you skip this for now, the app still works. You can turn on recent-call review from system settings whenever you need it.
Call log is empty or permission denied
File FTC Do Not Call Complaint
Protection status, setup progress, and quick actions.
Review call history and investigate suspicious numbers.
See what CallShield blocked and why.
Run a live check on any number before you answer or call back.
Manage rules, trusted contacts, and the local spam database.
Settings, diagnostics, release notes, and app health.
Type at least 2 characters to search known spam numbers and match reasons.
Use search to jump directly into known spam entries without leaving the current tab.
Paste a full number to find the matching spam record fast.
Try terms like robocall, scam, delivery, or hotline to scan match descriptions.
Even a short prefix or suffix is enough to narrow down suspicious campaigns.
Try a different number or keyword
Notifications when spam calls/texts are blocked
Rate calls as spam after they end
Alert when a received SMS contains a known malicious URL
Explains calls CallShield allowed because of safety rules
An SMS from %1$s contains a URL flagged by URLhaus as %2$s. Do not tap any links in this message.
CallShield allowed %s because the same number called repeatedly within a few minutes. Block it if this was unwanted, or mark it safe to trust future calls.
GBT v3 or LR v2 weights loaded
